articles.title: Google Home’s Gemini AI can handle more complicated requests
articles.summary: Google Home's Gemini AI has been upgraded to version 3.1, enabling it to handle complex multi-step tasks and combine multiple requests in a single command. The update improves event management and smart home assistant capabilities.

articles.title: Apple agrees to pay iPhone owners $250 million for not delivering AI Siri
articles.summary: Apple agreed to pay $250 million to settle a lawsuit alleging it misled customers about Apple Intelligence features on iPhone 15 Pro and iPhone 16 models. Eligible US purchasers can receive $25-$95 per device.

articles.title: Microsoft gives up on Xbox Copilot AI
articles.summary: Microsoft has discontinued Xbox Copilot AI, ending development on both mobile and console platforms. The decision follows new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma's reorganization of the Xbox team.

articles.title: Peter Sarlin’s QuTwo reaches $380M valuation in angel round
articles.summary: Finnish AI lab QyTw0, founded by Peter Sarlin, reached a $380M valuation after raising $29M in an angel round, reflecting strong investor interest in AI and sovereign European technology.

articles.title: Marc Lore says that AI will soon enable anyone open a restaurant
articles.summary: Wonder plans to transform robotic kitchens into AI-powered 'restaurant factories' enabling anyone to launch virtual food brands through simple prompts.

articles.title: SAP bets $1.16B on 18-month-old German AI lab and says yes to NemoClaw
articles.summary: SAP invests $1.16B in German AI startup Prior Labs and restricts customer AI agent access to select partners like Nvidia's NemoClaw.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure competing against AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing sparks backlash among developers. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar AI coding capabilities without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral San Francisco billboard hiring stunt featuring AI tokens. The creative puzzle-based recruitment campaign successfully attracted thousands of engineering candidates.

articles.title: Widely used Daemon Tools disk app backdoored in monthlong supply-chain attack
articles.summary: Daemon Tools disk mounting app was backdoored for a month, with malicious updates pushed from official servers affecting Windows versions 12.5.0.2421-12.5.0.2434. The supply-chain attack began April 8 and infected executables to run malware at boot time.

articles.title: Why Reddit blocked my daily visit to its mobile website
articles.summary: Reddit blocked mobile web access with an overlay forcing users to download the app, offering no skip option or alternatives. The company pushes app adoption by highlighting features like better search and personalized feeds.

articles.title: GameStop offers $56 billion for eBay, struggles to explain how it'll pay for it
articles.summary: GameStop made an unsolicited $55.5 billion offer to acquire eBay, proposing to leverage its physical retail locations for authentication and fulfillment. The bid faces skepticism as eBay's market cap is four times larger, and GameStop's financing plan remains unclear.

articles.title: The Download: inside the Musk v. Altman trial, and AI for democracy
articles.summary: Elon Musk is suing Sam Altman, alleging he was misled about OpenAI's transition to for-profit status. The landmark trial highlights tensions between two major AI figures over the company's direction.

articles.title: A blueprint for using AI to strengthen democracy
articles.summary: AI is reshaping democratic governance as the primary information interface, potentially strengthening or destabilizing institutions depending on design choices made today.

articles.title: Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: What it was like in the room
articles.summary: Elon Musk sued OpenAI and Sam Altman in Oakland court, claiming his funding was intended for a nonprofit, not a corporation. The high-stakes trial could impact OpenAI's planned IPO and represents a public feud between AI industry leaders.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent advancements in large language model architecture and training methods. The article explores the technical foundations and construction techniques behind these models.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra has been added to Hugging Face's inference providers, offering developers another option for deploying and running AI models. This integration expands accessibility to inference services on the popular ML platform.

articles.title: Introducing NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: Long-Context Multimodal Intelligence for Documents, Audio and Video Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a multimodal AI model supporting documents, audio, and video with extended context length for building intelligent document, audio, and video agents.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in artificial intelligence development. The conversation explores upcoming trends and innovations shaping the AI industry.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity across operations. From Hot Wheels to content creation, companies use AI for imaginative solutions and efficient workflows.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open-sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, enabling developers to build AI-powered agricultural solutions for sustainable farming practices and improved crop management.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners for a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition, challenging creators to explore AI's future through cinema.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in April 2026, featuring new developments and capabilities across its AI product portfolio.

articles.title: Reduce friction and latency for long-running jobs with Webhooks in Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces event-driven webhooks for Gemini API to reduce friction and latency in long-running jobs by eliminating inefficient polling through push-based notifications.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery, exploring how artificial intelligence can assist clinicians in patient care.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by decoupling local and global updates, improving fault tolerance and efficiency in large-scale model training across multiple nodes.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming the company misappropriated charity status. Evidence emerged that Musk's xAI trained models using OpenAI's technology.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's new GPT-5.5 with enhanced coding capabilities and safety monitoring, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and recent AI safety concerns.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text and screenshot generation. The episode discusses this alongside Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6 model updates.

articles.title: Physical AI raises governance questions for autonomous systems
articles.summary: Physical AI governance faces challenges as autonomous systems expand into robots and industrial equipment. Testing, monitoring, and control mechanisms must evolve as robotic installations surge globally.

articles.title: Google made agentic AI governance a product. Enterprises still have to catch up.
articles.summary: Google launched Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform at Cloud Next '26, making agentic AI governance a native product feature with built-in cryptographic identity tracking and Agent Gateway oversight for enterprise compliance.

articles.title: SAP: How enterprise AI governance secures profit margins
articles.summary: SAP emphasizes that enterprise AI governance ensures profit margins by prioritizing accuracy and control over statistical approximations. Organizations must transition from consumer-grade models to precision-focused systems with proper governance for production environments.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables LLMs to handle 100 million token context windows while maintaining accuracy, significantly advancing language model capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals AI coding assistants like Claude Code are inadvertently publishing sensitive API keys to public code repositories, posing security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that could expose millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, representing a significant supply chain security risk.

articles.title: Your job search is getting riskier, says LinkedIn - 9 ways to tell real listings from scams
articles.summary: LinkedIn reports one in three recruiters are impersonated by scammers. The article provides tips to identify fake job listings and protect yourself during your job search.

articles.title: All Linux gamers should take the latest Bazzite release seriously - here's why
articles.summary: Bazzite, a Linux distribution, offers an optimized out-of-the-box gaming experience for Linux gamers. The latest release improves performance and compatibility for gaming on Linux systems.

articles.title: Fedora 44 made me forget I was using Linux - in the best way
articles.summary: Fedora 44 offers numerous refinements making it one of the best versions, providing a seamless user experience that feels natural and intuitive.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: Non-deterministic agents produce varying outputs from identical inputs. Statistical guardrails help ensure consistent behavior and reliability.

articles.title: Agentic RAG Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Agentic RAG combines retrieval-augmented generation with autonomous agents to enable more dynamic decision-making. The article explains this concept across three difficulty levels for better understanding.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, a novel algorithmic suite for quantization and compression of large language models and vector search engines, essential for RAG systems.

articles.title: Discrete Time-To-Event Modeling – Predicting When Something Will Happen
articles.summary: Article introduces discrete time-to-event modeling fundamentals, covering time discretization, censoring, and life table methods for predicting when events occur.

articles.title: How to Make Claude Code Validate its own Work
articles.summary: The article discusses techniques for improving Claude Code's performance by implementing self-validation mechanisms. This enables the AI to check and verify its own generated code automatically.

articles.title: RAG Hallucinates — I Built a Self-Healing Layer That Fixes It in Real Time
articles.summary: A developer created a lightweight self-healing layer that detects and corrects hallucinations in RAG systems in real time, addressing reasoning failures rather than retrieval issues.

articles.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
articles.summary: NVIDIA and ServiceNow partner to develop autonomous AI agents for enterprises that can operate safely and consistently across real workflows, moving beyond simple prompts to complex task execution.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw agents have become a significant open-source phenomenon, reaching 100,000 GitHub stars by early 2026. The project enables organizations to build specialized AI systems using open models and datasets on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: It’s Gonna Be May: 16 Games Hit the Cloud This Month, With More NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Power
articles.summary: 16 games are launching on GeForce NOW this May, including AAA titles like Forza Horizon 6 and 007 First Light. RTX 5080 power expands to the Install-to-Play library for enhanced cloud gaming.

articles.title: How Hapag-Lloyd uses Amazon Bedrock to transform customer feedback into actionable insights
articles.summary: Hapag-Lloyd, a leading global shipping company, uses Amazon Bedrock to analyze customer feedback and convert it into actionable business insights, enhancing their digital customer experience.

articles.title: Streamlining generative AI development with MLflow v3.10 on Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI MLflow Apps now support MLflow v3.10, offering enhanced capabilities for generative AI development with improved experiment tracking, observability, and evaluation features.

articles.title: Introducing OS Level Actions in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser now supports OS-level actions, enabling AI agents to interact with system dialogs and native elements beyond the DOM that previous tools couldn't access.

articles.title: How to Build In-Vehicle AI Agents with NVIDIA: From Cloud to Car
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables developers to build advanced in-vehicle AI agents that transition from traditional rule-based systems to intelligent, multimodal AI capable of reasoning and planning. These agentic systems represent a fundamental shift in automotive cockpit technology.

articles.title: Building for the Rising Complexity of Agentic Systems with Extreme Co-Design
articles.summary: AI is transitioning from simple request-response interactions to complex agentic systems where agents autonomously call tools, spawn sub-agents, manage memory, and make independent decisions beyond predetermined sequences.

articles.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
articles.summary: NVIDIA's cuOpt Agent Skills optimize supply chain decisions by automating complex operations research problems traditionally requiring specialized teams. The solution addresses modern supply chain challenges like fluctuating demand and volatile costs through AI-driven decision-making.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing and sharing technology to reduce its carbon footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough innovations in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. The work uses feature attribution techniques to understand how LLMs make decisions.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders and noise models to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects in noisy conditions.
